description: Memory management, garbage collection, and ownership in programming languages.

distractors: the oldest objects in memory working toward newest
random sampling of the heap for statistical collection
objects explicitly marked by programmer annotations

facts.context: Reference counting (Python, Swift) has the weakness of:
facts.truth: cycles causing memory leaks without cycle detection
distractors: being too slow for any practical application
requiring manual memory management by the programmer
not working with object-oriented programming at all

facts.context: Generational GC exploits the hypothesis that:
distractors: older objects should be collected more frequently than new ones
all objects have equal lifetime distributions regardless of age
memory allocation patterns are completely random and unpredictable

facts.context: Rust's borrow checker enforces at compile time:
facts.truth: one mutable XOR multiple immutable references
distractors: garbage collection with zero runtime overhead
unlimited aliasing with runtime race detection
manual malloc/free like C requires

facts.context: RAII (Resource Acquisition Is Initialization) in C++ ensures:
facts.truth: resources released when objects go out of scope
distractors: garbage collection through reference counting only
manual resource cleanup calls required everywhere
memory leaks are impossible in all circumstances

facts.context: Escape analysis optimization allows the compiler to:
distractors: eliminate garbage collection entirely from programs
parallelize all memory allocations automatically
convert heap allocations to static allocations

facts.truth: bulk-freeing all objects in region together
distractors: requiring no memory management at all ever
making individual object deallocation faster
eliminating the need for any memory allocator

facts.context: Weak references help garbage collection by:
distractors: making all references automatically garbage collected faster
eliminating reference counting overhead completely
preventing any object from ever being collected

facts.truth: mutation through shared references with runtime checks
distractors: bypassing the borrow checker entirely with no safety checks
compile-time verification of all mutation patterns
only immutable data structures with no mutation ever
the program terminates and all memory is freed
every read access which triggers a full copy
garbage collection runs and copies all live objects
